an5.254:1.1: “Monks, there are these five forms of stinginess.
an5.254:1.3: Stinginess as to one’s monastery (lodgings), stinginess as to one’s family (of supporters), stinginess as to one’s gains, stinginess as to one’s status, and stinginess as to the Dhamma.
an5.254:1.4: These are the five forms of stinginess.
an5.254:1.5: And the meanest of these five is this: stinginess as to the Dhamma.”
